report_name: Celerium Launches 'DIB CyberDome' to Automate CMMC Compliance for Defense SMBs

tools.name: DIB CyberDome
tools.description: A cybersecurity platform launched by Celerium, tailored for small and mid-sized businesses (SMBs) in the U.S. Defense Industrial Base (DIB) to help them meet CMMC Level 2 compliance and defend against threats.

tools.name: Cyber Interceptor
tools.description: The core component of the DIB CyberDome platform that provides continuous network boundary monitoring and automated threat blocking, re-optimizing every 15 minutes.

tools.name: Elevated Defense System
tools.description: An AI-powered collective defense system within the DIB CyberDome platform that shares threat intelligence and coordinates responses across the DIB community.

tools.name: Security Information and Event Management
tools.description: A type of security solution that the DIB CyberDome platform aims to replace for SMBs to meet CMMC controls for monitoring.

tools.name: Security Orchestration, Automation, and Response
tools.description: A type of security solution that the DIB CyberDome platform aims to replace for SMBs to meet CMMC controls for threat response.

identities.name: Celerium
identities.description: The cybersecurity firm that launched the DIB CyberDome platform.

identities.name: Defense Industrial Base
identities.description: The target market for the DIB CyberDome platform, consisting of approximately 68,000 small and mid-sized businesses that are subcontractors to the U.S. Department of Defense.

identities.name: U.S. Department of Defense
identities.description: The U.S. government agency that requires Defense Industrial Base contractors to meet Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) standards.

identities.name: NIST
identities.description: The U.S. National Institute of Standards and Technology, whose Special Publication 800-171 forms the basis for CMMC Level 2 controls.
